response.reasoning: Candidate has 10+ years of full-stack experience, well exceeding the 6+ year requirement. Strong match on TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB from Optilyz and re:cinq roles. Current role at re:cinq explicitly involves AI agent infrastructure and MCP, matching the AI agent development requirement. Has Docker, Kubernetes (GISCollective), AWS (CDK at Optilyz) experience. Java experience present (Altom, robotic arms). TDD/craftsmanship focus aligns with code quality expectations. Gaps: no explicit Nest.js or Vitest mentioned, no Kotlin, no PostgreSQL (MySQL/MongoDB instead), limited Python evidence. DLang heavy background is tangential but shows language versatility. Has CS degree. Conference talks and workshops indicate strong communication. Overall a very strong fit with minor stack-specific gaps that are easily bridgeable.
strengths: 10+ years full-stack with TypeScript/Node.js/React and current AI-agent/MCP work directly matches stack
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development, public talks/workshops
Polyglot background (TS, Java, DLang, C/C++, Python-adjacent) plus Docker, Kubernetes, AWS CDK, MongoDB
concerns: No explicit Nest.js or Vitest experience listed in the primary stack
No Kotlin and limited PostgreSQL evidence (MongoDB/MySQL only)
Heavy DLang focus in several roles may indicate stack preferences misaligned with TS-first team

key_factors.factor: AI agent / MCP experience at current role
Missing Nest.js and Kotlin in stated stack

response.justification: Aisha presents as a seasoned full-stack engineer whose recent and current work aligns tightly with the role's TypeScript/Node.js/React core and the AI-agent requirement (MCP frameworks at re:cinq). Craftsmanship practices, architecture exposure, and cloud/containerization experience round out a strong profile. The gaps on Nest.js, Kotlin, and PostgreSQL are real but typical bridgeable items for an engineer with this breadth, warranting a confident interview recommendation.
